tasks.description: WebUIAdapter — read-only facade exposing pipeline state for HTTP routes
steps: Read PRD §17, §15, and §4 to understand what state the Web UI needs
Create src/web/adapter.ts
Define WebUIAdapter interface with methods: getStatus(), getPluginHealth(), getLiveMetrics(), getNetworkPolicy(), getUptime(), getMemoryUsage()
getStatus() returns: { state: 'starting' | 'running' | 'stopping' | 'stopped', startedAt: number | null }
getPluginHealth() returns array of: { alias: string, type: 'input' | 'processor' | 'aggregator' | 'output', status: 'ok' | 'error' | 'stopped', lastActivity: number | null, errorMessage?: string }

getLiveMetrics() returns: Map<string, { name: string, fields: Record<string, FieldValue>, tags: Record<string, string>, timestamp: bigint, quality: number }> keyed by metric name — latest value per unique metric name
getNetworkPolicy() returns: { mode: string, summary: string } | null
getUptime() returns milliseconds since start
getMemoryUsage() returns: { heapUsed: number, heapTotal: number, rss: number }
Implement PipelineWebUIAdapter class that implements this interface
Constructor takes PipelineOptions (for plugin metadata) and receives state updates

For live metrics: the adapter needs to tap the output broadcaster — add a registerMetricSink(callback) to PipelineRuntime that forwards copies of metrics flowing through the pipeline. Alternatively, the adapter subscribes to a Channel on the output broadcaster. Choose the simplest approach that doesn't modify the pipeline's data flow semantics.

For plugin health: expose plugin aliases and types from PipelineOptions. Status tracking requires adding onError/onActivity callbacks — for MVP, derive status from whether the plugin is in the options (ok) and track last metric timestamp per input alias.
For pipeline state: add a state field to PipelineRuntime ('starting' | 'running' | 'stopping' | 'stopped') updated during start() and stop(). Expose via a getter.
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/adapter.test.ts
Test: getStatus() returns 'running' after pipeline start, 'stopped' after stop
Test: getPluginHealth() returns correct aliases, types from PipelineOptions
Test: getLiveMetrics() returns latest metric per name after metrics flow through
Test: getNetworkPolicy() returns mode and summary when policy is set, null when absent
Test: getUptime() increases over time
Test: getMemoryUsage() returns positive numbers for heapUsed, rss
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: WebUIAdapter read-only pipeline facade (task 9.0)

tasks.description: Elysia HTTP server with static asset embedding and gzip compression
steps: Read PRD §17 (Technology section), §16 (port 8080, bind localhost), spike findings (Spike 5: asset embedding)
Add dependencies: bun add elysia @elysia/html @starfederation/datastar-sdk
Download Datastar RC.7 browser bundle to src/web/public/datastar.js: curl from jsdelivr
Download ECharts simple bundle to src/web/public/echarts.min.js: copy from node_modules or download
Create src/web/public/components/line-chart.js — ECharts web component wrapper from spike (Spike 4 findings)
Create src/web/server.ts
Define WebUIConfig type: { port: number, bind: string, enabled: boolean }
Default config: port 8080, bind '127.0.0.1', enabled true
Implement createWebServer(config, adapter): creates Elysia app with html() plugin
Implement static asset serving using import ... with { type: 'file' } for each public asset (spike finding: Bun.file() paths break in compiled binary)
Implement gzip compression for static assets: Bun.gzipSync() on first request, cache compressed result in memory
Set Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable on static assets
Set correct Content-Type headers for .js, .css files
Implement asset route: GET /static/* maps to embedded asset map
Return 404 for unknown static paths
Implement startWebServer(app, config) and stopWebServer(app) lifecycle functions
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/server.test.ts
Test: server starts on configured port and responds to requests
Test: GET /static/datastar.js returns 200 with correct Content-Type
Test: GET /static/echarts.min.js returns 200 with correct Content-Type
Test: GET /static/components/line-chart.js returns 200
Test: GET /static/nonexistent returns 404
Test: Cache-Control header is set on static responses
Test: Accept-Encoding: gzip returns gzipped response with Content-Encoding: gzip header
Test: server stops cleanly without errors
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: Elysia HTTP server with embedded static assets (task 9.1)

tasks.description: Dashboard page — server-rendered JSX shell with Datastar attributes
steps: Read PRD §17 MVP features, spike findings (Spike 1: JSX + Datastar attributes, Spike 3: element patching)
Create src/web/views/dashboard.tsx — main dashboard page component
Create src/web/views/layout.tsx — base HTML layout (head, scripts, styles)
Layout includes: Datastar RC.7 script (type=module), ECharts script, line-chart.js web component, inline CSS or styles.css
Dashboard sections as JSX components, all server-rendered with Datastar data-* attributes:
1. Header bar — 'CollatrEdge' title + pipeline status badge
2. Network policy banner — uses data-show to display only when policy is non-connected, colour-coded (amber local_network, red standalone)
3. Live metrics card — placeholder div with data-init='@get(/api/dashboard/stream)' for SSE, data-text bindings for values
4. Plugin health table — target div with id for patchElements morphing, traffic-light status dots
5. Trend charts section — 4x collatr-line-chart web components with data-effect bridges (spike finding: data-effect is the recommended pattern)
6. CSV export form — date inputs (from/to), download button, plain HTML form GET to /api/export
7. Footer — version, mode indicator
Use Datastar RC.7 colon syntax throughout: data-on:click, data-signals:name, data-init (NOT data-on-click, NOT data-on-load — spike finding: RC.7 breaking change)
Register GET / route in Elysia that renders the dashboard with initial state from WebUIAdapter
Pass initial values to JSX for server-side rendering (pipeline status, network policy, plugin list) so the page is immediately readable before SSE connects
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/views/dashboard.test.ts
Test: dashboard JSX renders to valid HTML string containing expected elements
Test: network policy banner present in output when policy mode is local_network
Test: network policy banner absent (data-show=false) when mode is connected
Test: live metrics section contains data-init attribute pointing to SSE endpoint
Test: chart web components present with data-effect attributes
Test: CSV export form has action pointing to /api/export
Test: all Datastar attributes use colon syntax (grep for data-on- to verify none use hyphen syntax)
Test: GET / returns 200 with Content-Type text/html
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: dashboard page with Datastar SSE shell (task 9.2)

tasks.description: SSE streaming endpoint — live metrics and status panel updates
steps: Read spike findings: Spike 2 (SSE streaming), Spike 3 (element patching), Phase 9 implications #6 and #7
Create src/web/routes/stream.ts
Implement GET /api/dashboard/stream SSE endpoint
Use ServerSentEventGenerator.stream() from @starfederation/datastar-sdk/web (spike finding: returns Response directly, Elysia passes it through)
Stream patchSignals every 1s: latest metric values from adapter.getLiveMetrics() as JSON-stringified signal object
Stream patchElements every 2s: server-rendered JSX fragments for status panel (pipeline state, uptime, memory) and plugin health table
Create src/web/views/fragments/status-panel.tsx — JSX component for the status panel fragment (must have id matching the target div in dashboard)
Create src/web/views/fragments/plugin-table.tsx — JSX component for plugin health table fragment
Both fragments are rendered to string (Kita JSX = plain string, spike finding: no renderToString needed)
Handle client disconnect gracefully: catch errors in the stream loop, break out of while(true)
Signal format: { metricName_fieldName: value, ... } flattened for Datastar text bindings
Guard against empty metrics: if adapter returns no metrics yet, send signals with placeholder values
Register route in the Elysia app
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/routes/stream.test.ts
Test: GET /api/dashboard/stream returns 200 with Content-Type text/event-stream
Test: response contains datastar-patch-signals events with metric data
Test: response contains datastar-patch-elements events with status HTML
Test: status panel HTML includes pipeline state and uptime
Test: plugin table HTML includes plugin aliases and status indicators
Test: stream sends updates at configured intervals (verify 2+ events received within 3s)
Test: stream handles adapter returning empty metrics gracefully
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: SSE dashboard stream with signals and element patches (task 9.3)

tasks.description: ECharts trend charts — historical data load + live append via SSE
steps: Read spike findings: Spike 4 (ECharts web component, bridge patterns), Phase 9 implications #9-#14
Create src/web/routes/chart-data.ts
Implement GET /api/chart/history?metric=<name>&from=<iso>&to=<iso> endpoint
Query LocalStoreOutput.query() for the time range and return JSON array of {timestamp, value} points
Default 'from' to 24 hours ago, default 'to' to now (PRD §17: last 24h trend)
Metric parameter selects which metric name to return — return the first numeric field value found
Limit response to 2000 points max — downsample if more (take every Nth point to fit within limit)
Implement GET /api/chart/metrics endpoint — returns list of available metric names from the local store for the chart selector
Update src/web/public/components/line-chart.js web component:
- Add connectedCallback: fetch /api/chart/history for initial data load
- Accept metric attribute to specify which metric to display
- Use data-effect bridge for live point append from SSE signals (spike finding: recommended pattern)
- Set animation: false (spike finding: required for 1Hz+ updates)
- Set yAxis min/max to dataMin/dataMax (spike finding: prevents anchoring at 0)
- Guard against initial signal value of 0 (spike finding: check timestamp > 1e12)
- Keep last 1000 live points in memory (shift oldest when exceeded)
- ResizeObserver for responsive resize
Update dashboard.tsx: wire chart web components with metric attributes and data-effect expressions
The adapter needs access to LocalStoreOutput for historical queries — add getLocalStore() to WebUIAdapter returning the store instance (or a read-only query interface)
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/routes/chart-data.test.ts
Test: GET /api/chart/history returns JSON array of data points
Test: default time range is last 24 hours
Test: metric parameter filters to correct metric name
Test: response is capped at 2000 points (downsample if needed)
Test: GET /api/chart/metrics returns list of metric names
Test: returns empty array when no data in store
Write unit tests for updated line-chart.js in test/unit/web/components/line-chart.test.ts (basic structure validation — full browser testing is out of scope)
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: trend charts with historical load and live SSE append (task 9.4)

tasks.description: CSV export endpoint with UTC and local timezone columns
steps: Read PRD §22 Scenario 4: CSV export of 1-hour range in <5 seconds, must include UTC AND local timezone timestamp columns
Read spike findings: Spike 6 (CSV export pattern)
Create src/web/routes/export.ts
Implement GET /api/export?from=<iso>&to=<iso>&tz=<timezone> endpoint
Parse from/to as ISO timestamps, convert to nanosecond bigints for LocalStoreOutput.exportCSV()
The existing LocalStoreOutput.exportCSV() returns CSV with a single timestamp column (nanoseconds). Extend or wrap it to include both UTC and local timezone columns per acceptance criteria.
Option A: Add a second CSV export method to LocalStoreOutput that includes formatted timestamps
Option B: Post-process the CSV output to prepend formatted timestamp columns. Simpler, keeps LocalStoreOutput unchanged.

Choose whichever approach is cleaner. The key requirement is: output has 'timestamp_utc' (ISO 8601 UTC) and 'timestamp_local' (ISO 8601 with timezone offset) columns in addition to or replacing the raw nanosecond timestamp.
tz parameter: optional, defaults to system timezone. Used for the local timestamp column. Accept IANA timezone names (e.g. 'Europe/London').
Return with Content-Type: text/csv and Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=collatr-edge-export-<timestamp>.csv
Handle empty result: return 204 No Content (not an empty CSV with just headers)
Update dashboard.tsx: CSV export form sends from/to as query params, optionally tz
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/routes/export.test.ts
Test: GET /api/export with valid time range returns CSV with correct headers
Test: CSV contains timestamp_utc and timestamp_local columns
Test: timestamp_utc is valid ISO 8601 in UTC (Z suffix)
Test: timestamp_local includes timezone offset
Test: Content-Type is text/csv
Test: Content-Disposition header triggers download with timestamped filename
Test: empty time range returns 204
Test: invalid from/to returns 400 with error message
Test: export completes in <5 seconds for reasonable data volume (acceptance criteria)
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: CSV export with UTC and local timezone columns (task 9.5)
Read PRD Appendix D §D.3 (certificate workflow) and §D.4 (TOFU) carefully
Create src/web/views/certificates.tsx — certificate management page
Create src/web/routes/certificates.ts — certificate API routes
Page sections:
1. Client Certificate — download button (.pem and .der), thumbprint display, validity dates, subject DN. This is the certificate operators need to add to their OPC-UA server's trust store.
2. Connection Status — per OPC-UA input: alias, endpoint URL, connection state (connected/rejected/disconnected), specific error message if rejected (e.g. BadCertificateUntrusted)
3. Server Certificates — per OPC-UA input: server certificate thumbprint, subject, validity. 'Trust' button for TOFU override.
Implement GET /api/certificates/client — returns client certificate info (thumbprint, subject, validity, paths)
Implement GET /api/certificates/client/download?format=pem|der — downloads the client certificate file
Implement GET /api/certificates/status — returns per-input connection status and server certificate info
Implement POST /api/certificates/trust — trusts a server certificate (writes to TOFU trust store). This is the only write operation in the MVP Web UI.
The adapter needs access to OPC-UA input plugin state — add getCertificateInfo() to WebUIAdapter that reads certificate paths from config and connection status from OPC-UA inputs
For MVP: certificate info may be partially available (client cert always readable from disk, connection status depends on OPC-UA inputs being configured)
If no OPC-UA inputs are configured, the page should say so clearly rather than showing empty tables
Navigation: add a link from the dashboard to /certificates, and from certificates back to dashboard
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/routes/certificates.test.ts
Test: GET /api/certificates/client returns certificate info when cert exists
Test: GET /api/certificates/client returns appropriate message when no cert configured
Test: GET /api/certificates/client/download?format=pem returns correct Content-Type and file content
Test: GET /api/certificates/status returns per-input connection status
Test: POST /api/certificates/trust with valid thumbprint succeeds
Test: certificates page renders with OPC-UA inputs listed
Test: certificates page renders gracefully with zero OPC-UA inputs
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: OPC-UA certificate helper page (task 9.6)

tasks.description: Config parsing, plugin factory wiring, and CLI integration
steps: Read PRD §16 (port 8080, bind localhost), §17 (Technology section), §10 (ingress rules)
Add [webui] section to config schema in src/core/config.ts
WebUI config schema: { enabled: boolean (default true), port: number (default 8080), bind: string (default '127.0.0.1') }
Parse [webui] section in parseConfig() — optional section, use defaults if absent
Add webui config to AgentConfig interface
Update plugin-factory.ts: if webui is enabled, create WebUIAdapter and Elysia server
Pass the adapter references that the factory has: PipelineOptions (for plugin metadata), LocalStoreOutput instance (for chart queries and CSV export), NetworkPolicy (for banner)
Add webServer and webAdapter fields to PipelineOptions (optional, only set when webui enabled)
Update run.ts: start web server after pipeline.start() succeeds, stop before pipeline.stop()
Log web UI URL at startup: 'Web UI available at http://{bind}:{port}'
Enforce network_policy.ingress.allow_local_webui: if false, skip web server creation entirely (log a message explaining why)
Update config-init.ts: add [webui] section to generated config templates (commented out for standalone, active for local_network and connected)
Update config-validate.ts: include webui config in validation output
Write unit tests in test/unit/web/config.test.ts (or add to existing config tests)
Test: config with [webui] section parses correctly
Test: config without [webui] defaults to enabled=true, port=8080, bind=127.0.0.1
Test: config with webui.enabled=false produces no web server
Test: ingress allow_local_webui=false skips web server
Test: config-init templates include [webui] section
Test: config-validate output shows webui settings
Update test/unit/cli/run.test.ts: verify web server starts and stops with pipeline
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: webui config parsing, plugin factory wiring, CLI integration (task 9.7)

tasks.description: Integration tests — HTTP endpoints, SSE streaming, CSV export, acceptance criteria
steps: Create test/integration/web-ui.test.ts
Test: full startup with mock inputs → GET / returns dashboard HTML with Datastar attributes
Test: GET /static/datastar.js returns JavaScript with correct Content-Type
Test: GET /static/echarts.min.js returns JavaScript

Test: GET /api/dashboard/stream returns SSE with patchSignals containing metric data within 3 seconds (covers acceptance criteria Scenario 1: live values within 60 seconds — here we verify the SSE mechanism works)
Test: GET /api/dashboard/stream returns SSE with patchElements containing status panel HTML
Test: SSE stream delivers 2+ signal events within 5 seconds
Test: GET /api/chart/history returns JSON array of data points from local store
Test: GET /api/chart/metrics returns list of metric names
Test: GET /api/export with populated local store returns CSV with timestamp_utc and timestamp_local columns
Test: CSV export Content-Disposition header includes filename
Test: GET /api/export with empty time range returns 204
Test: GET /certificates returns certificate page HTML
Test: network policy banner visible in dashboard HTML when mode is standalone
Test: network policy banner not visible when mode is connected
Test: webui enabled=false → no HTTP server listening (connection refused)
Test: full lifecycle — start pipeline with web UI, verify HTTP works, stop pipeline, verify HTTP stops
Create test/integration/web-ui-csv-perf.test.ts (separate file for the timing-sensitive test)
Test: insert 3600 metrics (1 per second for 1 hour) into local store, export 1-hour CSV in <5 seconds (acceptance criteria Scenario 4)
Run bun test test/unit test/integration — ALL tests pass
Commit: phase-9: integration tests for web UI endpoints and acceptance criteria (task 9.8)
